fields.summary: Nexus repository group not including newly created hosted repository
fields.description: We published our internal library to the maven-internal hosted repository but other teams cannot find it when using the maven-all group repository URL in their Maven settings. The library is visible when browsing nexus directly but maven dependency resolution against the group URL returns 404 for our artifact.

content.text: The artifact is definitely in maven-internal. I can browse it directly at nexus.company.com/repository/maven-internal. But when another team tries to resolve it via maven-all group they get 404.

Found it. The maven-internal hosted repository was not added to the maven-all group repository. When a new hosted repository is created it is not automatically included in existing group repositories - this must be done manually. Added maven-internal to maven-all group under Repository > Repositories > maven-all > Group > Members. The artifact is now resolvable via the group URL.
Confirmed - dependency resolving correctly via maven-all now. The fact that new repositories are not auto-added to groups is a Nexus gotcha that should be in the documentation.
